axioms.name: Boundedness
axioms.statement: Any persistent system has bounded resources; therefore it must compress.

axioms.name: Irreversible locking
axioms.statement: Compression becomes non-invertible when rollback cost greatly exceeds update budget.

axioms.name: Trace re-entry
axioms.statement: Non-invertible compression leaves traces that re-enter as constraints on future transitions.

axioms.name: Intuition signal
axioms.statement: Intuition is the system's marker of trace-dominant basins.
axioms.gloss: search is no longer worth it here

axioms.name: Cross-system convergence
axioms.statement: Different substrates under similar constraints converge to similar basins, yielding similar intuition-like correspondences.

negative_claim: These axioms do not claim a shared semantic ontology across species or models; they claim shared constraint-driven convergence.
